quality.rationale: 真实天体物理场景估算+密度对比直接推导+解答完整

origin.question: Problem 19.34. What is the order of magnitude of the influence of the cosmological constant on the celestial mechanics of the solar system if $\Lambda \sim 10^{-57} \mathrm{~cm}^{-2}$ ?
origin.solution: ## SolutionSolution 19.34. The effective $\rho_{\mathbf{c}}$ density corresponding to $\Lambda$ is$$\rho_{c} = \frac {\Lambda}{8 \pi} = \frac {1 0^{- 5 7} c m^{- 2}}{8 \pi} \approx 4 \times 1 0^{- 3 1} g m / c m^{3}.$$Typical densities in the solar system are$$\rho_{\text {b o d y}} \sim 1 \mathrm {g m} / \mathrm {c m}^{3}$$$$\rho_{\text {s o l a r s y s t e m}} \sim 1 0^{3 3} \mathrm {g m} / (1 0^{1 5} \mathrm {c m})^{3} \sim 1 0^{- 1 2} \mathrm {g m} / \mathrm {c m}^{3}.$$Thus, the effect of $\Lambda$ in the solar system, in order of magnitude is$$\frac {\rho_{c}}{\rho_{\text {s o l a r s y s t e m}}} \sim 1 0^{- 1 9}.$$
